title: UBS AM’s Zhao Sours on Treasuries as US Growth Beats Europe
summary: UBS Asset Management’s Kevin Zhao is planning to short Treasuries in a bet that the robust US economy will erode the haven appeal of owning government debt.

title: Circle Gets Street-Low Target as Stablecoin Competition Heats Up
summary: Circle Internet Group Inc. shares have lost more than three-quarters of their value from the highs seen just after their initial public offering last year and as more stablecoins come to market, one analyst is warning it could get worse.

title: Nasdaq Futures Tumble 1.5% as Chip Rout Deepens: Markets Wrap
summary: Nasdaq 100 futures tumbled 1.5% as the global selloff in chipmakers gathered pace, with traders rushing to exit positions in stocks that had fueled this year’s rally but whose valuations grew increasingly rich.

title: Mining Equipment Makers Sandvik, Epiroc Fall as Orders Slow
summary: Shares in mining equipment manufacturers Sandvik AB and Epiroc AB fell as a slide in precious metal prices since the start of the year weiged on orders.

title: Copper Follows Stock Markets Lower With AI Selloff Deepening
summary: Copper and other base metals retreated on Friday, tracking losses in equity markets as investor worries about the valuation of artificial-intelligence companies grow.

title: Chip Stocks Lead Selloff as Investors Question AI Assumptions
summary: Tech stocks are selling off and there’s no sign of any letup. A new AI model from a Chinese startup is one contributing factor.

title: Gold Heads for Second Weekly Drop as War Raises Fed Hike Fears
summary: Gold was on track for the biggest weekly loss since early June as renewed hostilities in the Middle East raised concerns that higher interest rates will be needed to contain war-driven inflation.

title: London Broker Howden Plots Giant Capital Raise on IPO Path
summary: Howden Group, the London-based insurance broker that’s chasing an initial public offering by 2030, is in talks to raise several billion pounds in new private capital as it seeks to maintain its breakneck expansion.

title: Dangote Taps $2.5 Billion in Private Share Placement Before IPO
summary: Aliko Dangote has nearly completed a $2.5 billion private stock placement for his refinery business, according to people familiar with the matter, as the company prepares for Africa’s largest initial public offering.

title: MSCI EM Index Heads for Correction as Chip Selloff Jolts Markets
summary: Emerging-market equities slumped, with a key gauge headed for a technical correction as mounting concerns over stretched AI valuations triggered a selloff in chip stocks across Asia.

title: Citi’s Manthey Says Broadening Requires Sharp Stock Rotations
summary: Sharp stock rotations are necessary for the equity rally to broaden beyond the tech sector, according to Citigroup Inc.’s Beata Manthey.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 17
summary: Oil headed for its biggest weekly advance since April as the escalating conflict between the US and Iran disrupted supply from the Middle East.

title: US Corporate Insiders Are Selling Stocks at a Near Record Pace
summary: US executives are selling shares at the second-fastest pace in more than 20 years, a classic red flag to some investors because it suggests people with the most corporate knowledge are wary about markets.

title: US Equity Futures Slide as Chip Selloff Prompts Sector Rotation
summary: US equity futures fell as a selloff in semiconductor stocks gathered steam, pushing investors to look for alternatives in other corners of the market.

title: Amundi Says Oil, Gas Crucial to EU’s Clean-Energy Transition
summary: Amundi SA wants the European Union to free asset managers to add oil and gas exposures to a new fund category intended to support the transition to a lower-carbon economy.

title: Zepto Is Said to Face Sharply Lower Valuation for India IPO
summary: Indian fast-delivery company Zepto Ltd. is seeking a valuation in an initial public offering sharply below its $7 billion peak as concerns over cash burn temper optimism around the industry and raise questions about the firm’s path to profitability.

title: Why Fed Independence Is Hanging by a Thread
summary: Last August, President Trump made the unprecedented choice of moving to fire Fed governor Lisa Cook. The administration claimed she was being terminated with cause, citing an ongoing investigation in alleged mortgage fraud committed by Cook. The legal battle between Cook and the administration has been tangled in the courts for the last year, eventually reaching the Supreme Court. This June, in a 5-4 decision, the court ruled in favor of Cook. However at the same time, in a different case, the c

title: Chip Stock Rout Deepens as TSMC Selloff Drives Taiwan Correction
summary: Taiwan stocks fell into a technical correction, amid broad losses in Asia’s chip sector as Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.’s results stoked worries over heavy spending and a weaker outlook for profitability.

title: Paschi Casts Doubts On Intesa Bid, Urges Studying BPM Plan
summary: Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena SpA expressed a number of concerns over a takeover bid by Intesa Sanpaolo SpA while highlighting perceived benefits in a broad, rival pitch presented by Banco BPM SpA.

title: Abu Dhabi’s ADIC Hires BlackRock Veteran to Lead Real Assets
summary: The Abu Dhabi Investment Council has hired a former BlackRock Inc. and Macquarie Group executive to lead its real assets business, underscoring the emirate’s ability to attract senior global investment talent despite the regional conflict.
